message:
Harden HTTP response headers against injection attacks

Extract header writing into a shared `write_header_line()` helper
that validates header names against RFC 9110 token characters and
strips CR/LF/NUL from header values to prevent header injection
(response splitting) attacks:

- `is_valid_header_name()`: rejects names containing non-token bytes
  (blocks names with embedded \r\n for CRLF injection)
- `sanitize_header_value()`: filters out \r, \n, and \0 bytes using
  Cow to avoid allocation when the value is already clean
- Replaces three inline extend_from_slice sequences (in Trailers::encode,
  write_full, and write_stream_head) with the single validated helper
- Adds comprehensive tests for both name rejection and value sanitization
  across all three response writing paths
- Updates Cargo.lock with dependency changes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
